# AI Coding Full Context

This site defines an AI-native software engineering workflow.

## Operating model

AI Coding requires small vertical slices, explicit context packs, independent AI reviewers, evidence packages, and human arbitration. AI implementers should not approve their own work. Reviewers should be isolated, read-only, and evidence-bound.


## Harness Engine

The site treats Harness Engine as the system layer around models and context. It includes context assembly, planning, tool runtime, workflow graphs, reviewer swarm, evidence store, policy/security, human gates, release orchestration, telemetry, and feedback learning. It synthesizes patterns from Anthropic harness design for long-running apps, OpenAI's practical guide to building agents, and LangChain/LangGraph's distinction between frameworks, runtimes, and harnesses.

## Acceptance model

The acceptance pipeline is:

1. Implementer Agent writes the slice.
2. Self Check runs minimal verification.
3. Reviewer Swarm runs independent reviews: spec, code, test, security, governance, UX, release.
4. Test Skeptic attacks the tests and proposes mutation scenarios.
5. Security Red Team looks for attack paths in auth, tenant, RBAC, data writes, secrets, CI/CD, dependencies, and logs.
6. Judge Agent deduplicates findings, assigns severity, and produces a short human decision report.
7. Human Arbiter accepts, rejects, requests fixes, or records a risk waiver.

## Reviewer output

Reviewers should output JSON with verdict, confidence, findings, missing_evidence, and human_decision_required. Findings require severity, type, file, line, claim, evidence, repro, suggested_fix, and status.

## Appendix sources

The appendix maps Claude Code Best Practices, OpenAI Building Agents, and LangChain framework/runtime/harness concepts into this site, especially verification-first workflows, explore-plan-code-review loops, rich context, subagents, permissions, MCP, hooks, skills, session management, non-interactive mode, and scaling patterns.

## Human role

Humans decide product behavior, risk acceptance, architecture direction, unresolved security concerns, and reviewer conflicts. Humans should not be forced to manually review all generated code before AI review.
